content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendments 1+3 (same root cause): Restored strict parsing in read_journal_from_path (src/adapters/fs.rs) — only the trailing line is tolerated as malformed (handles partial-write crash recovery), while non-trailing malformed lines now fail loudly with AppError::CorruptRecord including the file path and line number. This preserves the authoritative journal's integrity since forward-compatible event types are already handled by the #[serde(other)] Unknown variant on MilestoneEventType. Amendment 2: Added completion_round-based filtering to discard superseded PE mappings during reconciliation. In verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs), after filtering by active_bead_id and run_id, mappings are further narrowed to only those from the maximum completion_round for the current run — earlier rounds' PE decisions that were later fixed/rejected are excluded. In reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings, reconstruction now only scans the highest completion_round's aggregate (instead of all rounds), so superseded earlier-round PE amendments are not resurrected.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendments 1+3: Restored strict parsing in read_journal_from_path (src/adapters/fs.rs). Changed from unconditionally skipping all unparseable lines to a two-tier approach: collect all lines into a Vec first to know the total count, then for each malformed line check if it is the last line. Last-line malformed entries are tolerated with tracing::warn (partial-write crash recovery). Non-trailing malformed lines return AppError::CorruptRecord with file path and line number details.
2. [Completed] Amendment 2a: In verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs), after filtering bead_mappings by active_bead_id and run_id, added a second filter that finds the maximum completion_round across all filtered mappings and retains only mappings from that round. Earlier rounds' PE decisions that were superseded by later rounds are excluded from verification and commenting.
3. [Completed] Amendment 2b: In reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings (success_reconciliation.rs), changed from scanning all rounds in sorted order to only scanning the highest round. The latest_by_round HashMap still collects all rounds (for latest-by-created_at dedup), but only the maximum round key is used for reconstruction, preventing stale earlier-round PE amendments from being resurrected.
4. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to auto-fix minor formatting differences introduced by the edits.
5. [Completed] Ran cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (lib, integration, unit, doc-tests)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (fs.rs, success_reconciliation.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendments 1+3 verified: read_journal_from_path at fs.rs:3064-3107 now collects lines into Vec, checks is_last_line = (i + 1 == line_count), tolerates only trailing malformed line with tracing::warn, returns AppError::CorruptRecord { file: path.display(), details: line N is malformed } for non-trailing malformed lines. The #[serde(other)] Unknown variant on MilestoneEventType handles forward-compatible event types (valid JSON with unknown type) independently.
- Amendment 2 verified: verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success filters bead_mappings to max completion_round after run_id filter. If max_round is Some(N), only mappings with completion_round == Some(N) are retained. reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings now uses rounds.last().copied().into_iter().collect() to only scan the highest round, preventing earlier-round PE amendments from being reconstructed.
- Files modified: src/adapters/fs.rs (strict journal parsing with trailing-line tolerance), src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (completion_round filtering in verify + reconstruction)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- One flaky integration test (checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_includes_*) occasionally fails due to git timing; unrelated to this change and non-reproducible in isolation.
- The trailing-line tolerance in read_journal_from_path means a crash that produces a partial write on the last line will lose that single event. This is an accepted tradeoff — the alternative (failing the entire journal read) is worse for availability, and the partial write represents an uncommitted event.
- The completion_round filter in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success uses Option::max which naturally handles None values (legacy mappings without completion_round are excluded when any round-stamped mappings exist). If all mappings have completion_round: None (legacy data only), the filter is a no-op and all mappings pass through.
